title: Sour Pillowy Syrian Eggplant Pilaf
description: A sour and pillowy solo dinner dish from Syria featuring confit tomato and blue food coloring, perfect for a dairy free diet. This unique pilaf combines the flavors of the Middle East with a twist of blue color.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the eggplant cubes with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and 1/4 teaspoon of blue food coloring until they are evenly coated.
Spread the eggplant cubes on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until they are tender and lightly browned.
In a large skillet, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Stir in the confit tomato, lemon juice, and roasted eggplant cubes.
Cook the pilaf for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the flavors are combined and the eggplant is heated through.
